summary: News articles published within the previous 24 hours have outlined several specific developments in the small UAS domain. Ukraine has unveiled the Gnome-NS, a new ground-based "mother drone" designed to carry and deploy FPVs, and also introduced the Gnom-ND wheeled-tracked FPV carrier drone. Russian forces have deployed fiber-optic FPV drones in Kharkiv for the first time since the start of the full-scale invasion, with one hitting the city and another striking Perelyub village near the Chernihiv border. Ukrainian forces used FPV drones to knock out a Russian Kasta-2E2 radar in Zaporizhzhia and injured a 67-year-old civilian in Orikhiv. Ukraine’s I-SEE system is now tracking Russian drones in real time without emitting radio signals, and TAF Industries’ revenue grew 80% last year, with a new interceptor expected on marketplaces this spring.
